label: Hamas Mass Rocket Barrage (Oct 7, 2023 — Northern)
notes: Hamas launched ~3,000 rockets in opening salvo of October 7 attack, targeting Israeli communities from Negev to Tel Aviv area. Iron Dome intercepted majority but many got through.

label: Hamas Rockets — Sderot / Ashkelon (Oct 7)
notes: Southern Israeli cities Sderot and Ashkelon hit by rockets to suppress air defenses while ground infiltrators breached the fence. Short-range rockets overwhelmed Iron Dome's geometric firing solutions.

label: IAF Strikes — Gaza City (Oct 7–8, 2023)
notes: Israel Air Force launched hundreds of precision strikes on Hamas military targets in Gaza City within hours of the October 7 attack. Targeted Hamas command nodes, rocket launch sites, tunnel entrances, and military facilities.

label: IAF Strikes — Rafah (Oct–Nov 2023)
notes: Israeli Air Force strikes on Rafah targeting Hamas command infrastructure and tunnels. Residential buildings destroyed in large numbers given the density of the city.

label: Hamas Long-Range Rockets — Tel Aviv
notes: Hamas fired long-range rockets toward the greater Tel Aviv metropolitan area, including central and northern Israel, for the first time at scale. Iron Dome intercepted the vast majority.

label: Iran Direct Attack — 300+ Drones & Missiles (Apr 13–14, 2024)
notes: First-ever direct Iranian military attack on Israel: 170 drones, 120 ballistic missiles, 30 cruise missiles. 99% intercepted by Israel, US, UK, France, and Jordan. One hit an airbase in the Negev causing minor damage. Revolutionary Guards child killed near base.

label: Israel Limited Strike on Iran (Apr 19, 2024)
notes: Israel conducted limited retaliatory strike near Isfahan, Iran, reportedly using small drones. Iran downplayed the strike; Israel did not officially claim it. Designed to demonstrate reach without triggering full escalation.

label: Hezbollah Rocket Fire — Northern Israel (Oct 2023–Nov 2024)
notes: Hezbollah opened sustained 'support front' from Lebanon, firing thousands of anti-tank missiles, rockets, and drones at northern Israel from October 8, 2023 through November 2024 ceasefire. Caused evacuation of 60,000+ northern Israeli residents.

label: IDF Strikes on Hezbollah — Lebanon
notes: Escalating Israeli airstrikes on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon throughout 2024, culminating in massive campaign in September-October 2024 killing top Hezbollah leadership including Hassan Nasrallah. IDF also conducted brief ground operations in southern Lebanon.

label: Hamas Rockets — Jerusalem
notes: Hamas fired rockets toward Jerusalem, including toward the Al-Aqsa Mosque area, in the opening hours of the October 7 attack. Iron Dome intercepted most. One fell near the outskirts.

label: Hamas Anti-Tank Missiles — Border Communities
notes: Hamas used Kornet anti-tank missiles and other AT weapons to destroy IDF Merkava tanks and vehicles attempting to respond to the October 7 breach. Also used against civilian vehicles fleeing communities.

label: IAF Strikes — Khan Younis Campaign (Jan–Apr 2024)
notes: Sustained IAF campaign targeting Hamas military infrastructure, tunnel networks, and alleged command posts in Khan Younis. Accompanied by major IDF ground offensive in January 2024. Heavy civilian casualties resulted.

label: IAF Rafah Campaign (May–Oct 2024)
notes: Sustained aerial and ground operations in Rafah from May 2024. Targeted Hamas tunnel network under the Philadelphi Corridor and remaining Hamas brigades. Over 1 million civilians displaced from Rafah during operations.

label: Hamas Long-Range Rockets — Haifa Area
notes: Hamas fired M-302 and other long-range rockets toward Haifa and northern Israel at various points during the conflict. Iron Dome and Arrow systems intercepted most. Demonstrated Hamas's expanded rocket range.

label: World Central Kitchen Convoy Strike Route (Apr 1, 2024)
notes: IDF drone strikes on three WCK vehicles traveling from Deir al-Balah. The convoy had coordinated its route with the IDF. Three separate strikes hit different vehicles. Seven aid workers killed. Led to firing of two IDF officers.

label: Hamas Rockets — Ashdod
notes: Major Israeli port city of Ashdod targeted repeatedly by Hamas rockets. Iron Dome intercepted most but some fell in the city causing casualties and damage. Disrupted port operations temporarily.

label: IAF Strike on Jabalia Camp (Oct 31, 2023)
notes: Large Israeli airstrikes targeting Hamas commander Ibrahim Biari in Jabalia refugee camp. Created massive crater, destroyed surrounding buildings. At least 126 killed. IDF confirmed targeting Hamas commander.

label: Houthi Maritime Attack Zone (Red Sea)
notes: Houthi forces attacked over 100 commercial ships in the Red Sea citing Gaza solidarity. Seized Galaxy Leader. Sank Rubymar cargo ship. US-UK Operation Prosperity Guardian conducted extensive strikes on Yemen in response.

label: Al-Ahli Hospital Explosion (Oct 17, 2023)
notes: Contested: Hamas blamed Israeli airstrike, killing 500+ (later reduced to ~100-471); US and Israel said misfired PIJ rocket. UN commission found projectile from within Gaza. One of war's most disputed incidents.

label: Sinwar Elimination (Oct 16, 2024)
notes: IDF infantry patrol encountered Yahya Sinwar (Hamas supreme leader) in a building in Tal al-Sultan, Rafah. Engaged and killed him. Subsequent tank fire destroyed the building. Sinwar identified by DNA and fingerprints.

label: Hamas Tunnel Network (North–South)
notes: Hamas's 'Metro' underground tunnel network estimated at 500+ km spanning the entire Gaza Strip. IDF discovered and destroyed major portions during ground operations. Used for logistics, command, and civilian hostage holding.

label: IDF Ground Advance — Encircle Gaza City (Oct–Nov 2023)
notes: IDF ground forces advanced from multiple points on the Israeli border to encircle Gaza City from north, east, and south simultaneously. Heavy armored and infantry forces cut the city off from the south via the Netzarim Corridor.

label: Netzarim Corridor Establishment (Nov 2023)
notes: IDF established east-west military corridor through central Gaza at the former Netzarim settlement site, bisecting the Strip and severing north Gaza from the south. Civilian crossings strictly controlled; IDF maintained presence.

label: IDF Philadelphi Corridor Control (May–Oct 2024)
notes: IDF established military control over the Philadelphi Corridor — the narrow strip along Gaza's border with Egypt — claiming it was vital to stopping Hamas weapons smuggling. Became major sticking point in ceasefire negotiations; Egypt and Hamas demanded withdrawal.

label: Hamas Drone Attacks on Israeli Communities
notes: Hamas deployed explosive drones alongside the October 7 rocket barrages and ground infiltration, targeting IDF observation towers, radar systems, and border communities. Drones suppressed Israeli early warning capabilities.

label: IDF Khan Younis Ground Advance (Jan 2024)
notes: IDF ground forces advance from the corridor into Khan Younis from multiple directions. Division 98 and Division 36 conducted major urban combat operations hunting Hamas senior leadership including Sinwar. Fighting lasted until late April 2024.

label: IDF Advance into Rafah (May–Jul 2024)
notes: IDF ground forces advance into Rafah from the north and east. Seized Palestinian side of Rafah crossing May 7. Heavy urban fighting in Rafah city neighborhoods. Tunnel systems found under residential areas and Philadelphi Corridor.

label: Hamas Tunnel Smuggling Route (Philadelphi)
notes: Hamas's primary weapons smuggling route from Egypt into Gaza via underground tunnels beneath the Philadelphi Corridor. Israel said hundreds of tunnels existed; destroying them was stated military objective justifying Rafah operation.

label: IDF Second Shifa Hospital Operation (Mar 2024)
notes: IDF returned to Al-Shifa Hospital complex with a two-week operation claiming 900 militants killed/captured and significant weapons found. WHO and UN said hospital effectively destroyed and non-functional following the operation.

label: Nuseirat Hostage Rescue (Jun 8, 2024)
notes: IDF special forces and infantry daylight raid on Nuseirat refugee camp freed 4 hostages (Noa Argamani, Almog Meir Jan, Andrey Kozlov, Shlomi Ziv). At least 274 Palestinians killed in the operation. Significant IDF casualties also occurred.

label: Haniyeh Assassination — Tehran (Jul 31, 2024)
notes: Hamas Political Bureau chief Ismail Haniyeh killed by explosion at his Tehran guesthouse. Iran and Hamas blamed Israel; Israel did not confirm. Targeted the main Hamas negotiator for ceasefire talks. Led to warnings of regional escalation.

label: Mohammed Deif Strike — Khan Younis (Jul 13, 2024)
notes: Massive IDF airstrike targeting Hamas military commander Mohammed Deif and commander Rafa Salama in Khan Younis. At least 90 Palestinians killed. Both commanders confirmed killed weeks later. Key targeted killing of the war.

label: US Temporary Pier Aid Route (May 2024)
notes: US military temporary floating pier (JLOTS) constructed to deliver aid by sea to Gaza. Pier faced repeated operational problems due to weather, was towed for repairs multiple times, and ultimately delivered very limited aid before being dismantled.

label: Hamas Rockets During Ceasefire Collapse (Dec 1, 2023)
notes: Hamas fired rockets toward Israeli territory on December 1, which Israel cited as the reason for ending the November ceasefire. Hamas said Israel fired first; Israel said Hamas broke the truce.

label: IDF Returns to Jabalia (Jan 2025)
notes: IDF launched new ground operation in northern Jabalia area, saying Hamas was regrouping. Tens of thousands of Palestinians ordered to evacuate again. Operation continued through the ceasefire announcement. Showed Hamas resilience in north Gaza.

label: Hostage Handover Route — Gaza City (Jan 2025)
notes: Route used for hostage transfers in January 2025 ceasefire. Hostages handed over in Gaza City areas in coordinated transfers witnessed by Red Cross. Massive crowd scenes. Israeli forces waited at Gaza border for handovers.

label: IDF Resumes Airstrikes (Mar 2025)
notes: After Phase 2 ceasefire negotiations stalled, IDF resumed airstrikes across Gaza in March 2025. Strikes targeted Hamas military infrastructure rebuilt during ceasefire period. Reignited international protests and UN condemnation.

label: US/UK Strikes on Houthi Yemen Targets (Jan 2024–)
notes: US and UK launched Operation Prosperity Guardian strikes on Houthi military targets in Yemen beginning January 11, 2024, in response to attacks on Red Sea shipping. Strikes continued throughout 2024–2025 targeting Houthi radar, missile storage, and drone facilities.

label: Israel Retaliatory Strike on Iran (Oct 26, 2024)
notes: Israel conducted significant retaliatory strikes on Iranian military targets following Iran's October 1 ballistic missile attack. Struck Iranian air defense radars, missile production sites, and military infrastructure near Tehran. Israel called it 'precise and powerful'; Iran downplayed the damage.

label: Exploding Pager Operation — Lebanon (Sep 17, 2024)
notes: Thousands of AR-924 pagers used by Hezbollah exploded simultaneously across Lebanon in an attributed Israeli intelligence operation. 12 killed, 2,800 wounded. Next day walkie-talkies also exploded (20 more killed). Unprecedented supply chain interdiction.
